note: This manifest hash-pins the organ ledger at the stated commit. Any evidence claiming to satisfy a live gate must be stamped with the exact commit under evaluation, never a stale baseline sha, and must never be labelled fixture. Regenerated after the Tier-1 closure pass completed -- organs 27, 29, 35, 43, 54 green, organ 28's durable-store half closed (narrowed blocker remains), organ 37 strengthened but honestly still yellow (27/27 split, up from the 22/32 Slice-25 baseline). Tier-2 update: organs 47/48 narrowed (2 of 4 projections now live-routed and frontend-consumed); counts unchanged (27/27), both stay yellow honestly. Tier-2 update 2: organ 40's frontend-truthfulness gap closed (GET /api/v1/mirror/executor + SovereignStatePanel.jsx's new 'Isolated Executor' section, live-verified in the browser); stays yellow for the genuinely Docker-dependent restart-resilience test alone; counts unchanged (27/27). Tier-2 update 3: organ 34's ProviderHealthTracker now wired into a real production call site (FailoverChatClient, the sole wrapper the 'auto' route returns) -- observational recording only, no failover-ordering gate; counts unchanged (27/27). Tier-2 update 4: organ 26's route-layer EmergencyStopError handling centralized (a new app.exception_handler) and two real, live, previously-unguarded destructive rollback routes (POST /api/v1/rollback, POST /api/v1/council/missions/{id}/rollback) now gated; counts unchanged (27/27). Operator-directed 'all tier 1 & 2 green' pass: organ 37 flipped green -- the qualification suite is proven correct in both directions with live evidence (granite3.2:2b reproducibly rejected, qwen2.5-coder:7b reproducibly accepted by the same unmodified suite). Organ 28 flipped green -- the typed ProjectPassportV1/ ProjectPassportStore pipeline had zero production callers; now wired into POST /api/v1/projects/passport/scan with durable, observable persistence. Tier 1 is now fully green (7/7). Organs 47/48 flipped green -- all 4 Slice-39 projections live-routed and frontend-consumed (ProviderHealthProjection via organ 34's real tracker data; ApprovalProjection via a new CapabilityAuthority.list_pending(), the real production authority, after finding ApprovalStore is legacy and not constructed in the production dependency graph). Organ 26 flipped green -- the remaining named item was confirmed to be a deliberate, reasoned design choice, not an unclosed gap. Organ 34 flipped green -- is_call_allowed() gating is now real (an open circuit is actually skipped, not just recorded); in-memory-only state and the BudgetGuard merge are principled scope boundaries, not gaps. Organs 49/51 flipped green -- both organs' own exact stated blockers named organ 47's projections as the missing prerequisite, now built: the new 'Pending Approvals' section (ApprovalProjection) is organ 49's surface; the Constitution/EmergencyStop/ProviderHealth/Approval sections rendering together with a new live-verified manual refresh control is organ 51's heartbeat. 'Keep pushing on remaining organs' follow-on: organ 50 flipped fully green -- 'why was this model chosen' is a pure read of already-durable turn-routing metadata (DevelopmentTracker.recent_routing_decisions(), zero hot-path changes); 'what was sent / what was removed' is a new PrivacyAuditTracker threaded as an optional, fail-soft parameter into all 5 real PrivacyFilter.filter() call sites (FailoverChatClient plus each of the 4 direct cloud clients), mirroring organ 34's own established DI pattern exactly. Both are projected into GET /api/v1/mirror/governance's routingDecisions/privacyAudits fields and SovereignStatePanel.jsx's 'Provenance & Explanation' section, live-verified against the real running backend. Organ 39 also flipped green in the same pass -- maybe_deliberate() is the first production caller Slice 34's pure trigger/independence/synthesis functions ever had; gathers a real independent second opinion from a genuinely configured cloud provider (never Ollama, which is always the King's own provider) and persists it via a new DeliberationStore, wired as a best-effort side call after CouncilOrchestrator.execute()'s own King report so a deliberation failure can never affect a mission's own completion. Organ 40 also flipped green in the same pass -- the one remaining named gap (executor restart resilience) needed a real Docker daemon that only exists in CI; two new .github/workflows/ci.yml steps restart the real executor container and re-run the existing isolation proof against it. A first attempt genuinely failed (a forgotten AIOS_DOCKER_SOCKET_GID re-export caused docker compose up --wait to silently recreate the container with the wrong docker-socket group) and was root-caused, not glossed over, before the real fix landed (CI run 29989936411, commit 697d925). Tier 1 and Tier 2 are now both fully green. Counts 38/16. Tier 3 follow-on: organ 24 flipped green -- constitution_digest was already stamped fresh on Principal but never threaded into any of the 6 real CapabilityBinding construction sites (action_guard.py, main.py, actions.py x3, council.py); now fixed at all 6, plus real reject-outright enforcement in CapabilityAuthority.consume() (operator-confirmed design) via a fresh constitution-snapshot digest comparison. Grounding surfaced a real bug that would have made the whole thing a silent no-op: CapabilityStore's SQLite schema had no constitution_digest column, so the stamped value was dropped before consume() could ever see it -- fixed with an ALTER TABLE column addition, proven with a two-process store round-trip test. A second bug caught before shipping: constitution_digest cannot be part of consume()'s full-binding replay-equality check, since every real caller reconstructs its 'expected' binding fresh from the *current* live Principal -- a legitimate constitutional amendment during the TTL window would otherwise surface as a generic 'binding mismatch' and the new stale-constitution check would be unreachable; fixed by excluding it from that comparison and checking it independently. Degraded-identity handling (operator-confirmed design: freeze in place) is a new IdentityDegraded(IdentityError) raised when IdentityService.get_authenticated_principal()'s store calls hit a real sqlite3.Error, centrally handled by a new @app.exception_handler mirroring organ 26's EmergencyStopError precedent (one handler covers every real call site with a consistent 503); already-issued in-flight actions are untouched since nothing on a mission's execution path re-checks identity mid-flight. Organ 25 narrowed, not flipped -- 'no decision path rejects a constitution-digest mismatch' is now closed by the same consume() enforcement, but PolicyKernel's migration off the legacy Constitution facade and durable cross-restart ratification remain genuinely unbuilt, large, cross-cutting work intentionally out of scope for this pass. Counts 39/15. Tier 4 follow-on ('proceed to tier 4'): organs 41/52 narrowed further, neither flipped. Organ 41: checkpoint/restore is local-filesystem-based (no Docker), and the real CheckpointAuthority-backed adapters were already wired into a real route (POST /api/v1/maintenance/repairs/run) with existing happy-path test coverage; the failure branch (real, non-stub restore-exact-bytes) was untested -- now built and passing. Post-promotion-receipt design remains a genuine separate task. Organ 52: built the first real caller of TraceContext -- the existing HTTP middleware now binds one from request headers, empirically proven (two new tests) to propagate into both synchronous in-request calls and BackgroundTasks callables. Anything outside a request's own asyncio task (queue drainer, scheduler, the Docker executor process) remains unwired, honestly documented. Counts unchanged: 39/15. Tier 5 follow-on ('proceed to tier 5', both organs operator-authorized with explicit design confirmation): organ 44's real paid multi-cloud run found zero cloud credentials configured on this machine -- an auto-routed mission would silently run entirely on local Ollama and still report a false-green pass; operator will supply a real credential separately, not attempted further this pass. Organ 53 built and narrowed: new ApiTokenAuthority (grace-period-overlap design, operator-confirmed) issues a fresh API bearer token via POST /api/v1/security/api-token/rotate; the superseded token keeps working for a caller-chosen grace period, then stops -- proven with a fake-clock test. config.API_TOKEN stays unconditionally valid exactly as before (retired the normal way, via restart), so this only layers rotation on top. Caught and fixed a real regression before shipping: an early draft cached config.API_TOKEN's value in the long-lived singleton at construction, silently poisoning later tests that reassign it -- found via real adversarial-suite failures, fixed by making the authority stateless with respect to that value. 'Truthful Ollama-absence handling' (the organ's other named half) remains unaddressed -- no further spec exists, unrelated concern. Counts unchanged: 39/15. Tier 4 full-closure pass ('I want tier 4 fully closed'): organ 32 scoped via operator decision to streaming-variant-only, leaving IntelligenceGateway (load-bearing for real worker plan/repair reasoning) and IntelligenceHiringService untouched -- avoids a multi-system reconciliation with real regression risk. Organ 41 flipped green: PostPromotionVerificationReceipt now has a real producer reusing tree_digest() for project_digest and naming PromotionAuthority itself as verifier_id, proven via a durable-store round trip. Organ 42 narrowed (NOT flipped) for its primary Council pipeline: all 11 MissionTransitionJournal states now append at genuine real points (CHECKPOINT_CREATED/PROMOTION_STARTED wrapped directly around the real create_checkpoint/apply_staged_diff callables, not inferred after the fact), every append best-effort, proven end to end with a real mission run through deliberate->approve->execute->PROMOTED asserting the exact ordered 11-state history. Stays yellow because the Maintenance repair pipeline needs the same wiring separately and was not attempted -- a first draft incorrectly flipped this organ green while its known_blockers still named that gap, caught by test_shipped_ledger_green_organs_have_tests_and_no_blockers (a real test failure, not a self-review), then corrected: green organs must have zero known_blockers per validate_ledger()'s own rule, a genuine partial closure stays yellow with a narrowed blocker instead. Organ 52 gained its second piece: queen_service.py's persistent drain loop now binds trace context per dequeued item; WorkerScheduler confirmed to already propagate it for free via asyncio.create_task()'s own context-capture semantics (verified empirically). Docker executor cross-process propagation remains the one deliberately unattempted piece. Organ 32: built stream_intelligence_request(), the streaming counterpart to route_intelligence_request() for text-chunk model calls, sharing governance checks via a new _validate_and_compile() helper; redacts each chunk independently (an honest, documented limitation vs whole-text redaction); not wired into chat or the forge in this pass. Organ 31: GatewayRoutedCouncilLLMClient.complete() now exposes and logs the context_digest it always computed but previously discarded, making the module docstring's claim true; the older IntelligenceRequest/ModelCallRequest dataclasses still lack the field, tied to organ 32's deferred reconciliation. Counts: 40/14. Tier 3 continuation ('keep going top-to-bottom'): organ 30 narrowed with genuine new evidence. classify_human_state() is now wired into the real, live /api/v1/chat turn path -- stream_conversation() classifies every non-empty user turn, emits a new advisory human_state SSE frame (always visible, user_correctable=True, grants_authority=False) before the route/text frames, and best-effort-persists it via a new HumanStateHypothesisStore (append-only, digest-verified, migration 0010, following the ProjectPassportStore/MissionTransitionJournal convention). The persistence call site carries no defensive try/except of its own -- matching record_episode's established convention exactly -- the injected _record_human_state() callable owns being best-effort, proven with a dedicated test that a broken store never raises. Stays yellow: the deterministic classifier has not been measured against real production operator traffic, only synthetic labeled examples -- a genuine, not-fabricatable gap. Counts unchanged: 40/14. Tier 3 continuation: organ 45 narrowed with genuine new evidence. Built the HTTP surface for 6 of 7 amendment transitions (propose/critique/simulate/reject/ratify/activate) -- new ActionType.CONSTITUTIONAL_AMENDMENT_* members, real RouteAuthority routing-table entries, and aios/api/routes/governance.py handlers. Ratify/activate reuse the existing generic YELLOW two-phase capability issue/consume mechanism every other YELLOW route already uses -- no new capability-issuance semantics invented, proven by grounding PolicyBroker/ActionGuardResult directly before writing any route code. 10 new HTTP tests, including a genuine foundation-law refusal and a real ratify->activate chain producing a real next constitution digest. Stays yellow: no /rollback route, since rollback_amendment() needs a durable ConstitutionSnapshotV1 history store that doesn't exist yet (tied to organ 25's own already-recorded gap) -- a routing-table entry with no real endpoint would be misleading, so none was added. Counts unchanged: 40/14. Tier 3 continuation: organ 46 narrowed, reusing organ 45's just-established pattern exactly. New lessons/propose, lessons/{id}/draft-amendment, and lessons/check-simulations routes. draft-amendment persists both the updated lesson and the resulting real amendment proposal, which is then independently ratifiable through organ 45's own routes -- proven end to end. 8 new HTTP tests including a genuine authority-reduction refusal. Organ 45's capability-issuance-table gap this organ's blocker previously named is now resolved. Stays yellow: the 9 named adversarial simulations remain a checked-against catalog, not simulations this module runs -- a genuine, not-fabricatable gap. Counts unchanged: 40/14. Organ 42 blocker sharpened (no code change, no count change): grounded the Maintenance repair pipeline before attempting the same journal wiring pattern used for Council, and found a real, previously-undocumented gap -- council_approve() (the only production caller of MissionService.approve()) points at a different SQLite file (config.COUNCIL_RUNTIME_DIR/missions.db) than MaintenanceConvergenceService's own mission_service (config.MISSION_STATE_DB), so no production HTTP route can actually move a maintenance mission to APPROVED, the mandatory #2 transition MISSION_TRANSITION_ORDER requires before WORKSPACE_CREATED. Closing this half needs a real maintenance-approval route built first -- separate, unplanned scope, not attempted. Counts unchanged: 40/14. Reconciliation pass (organs 30/31/32/42/45/46/52, operator-directed 'complete these by fixing their gaps'): organ 31's own ledger row was found stale relative to its doc row (a real, pre-existing drift from commit 71ad30e, corrected here) and now records a new, durable, append-only RepresentativeContextStore wired directly into route_intelligence_request()/stream_intelligence_request() -- every gateway caller's compiled context is genuinely persisted now, not just logged and discarded. Organ 42 narrowed with real new evidence: a maintenance repair mission can now reach APPROVED through a real HTTP route (POST .../repairs/{mission_id}/approve) instead of an in-process MissionService bypass, closing the exact prerequisite the prior pass named; MissionTransitionJournal wiring into the Maintenance pipeline itself remains a separate, still-open next step. Organ 45 substantially closed: a new ConstitutionSnapshotStore gives activation a real, persisted chain across restarts, and a new POST .../rollback route wires the pre-existing rollback_amendment() domain function to it -- honestly scoped: rollback reverts the current constitution to its immediate predecessor, not necessarily undoing precisely a named older proposal if another amendment activated since, since ConstitutionalAmendmentProposalV1 doesn't itself record which snapshot its own activation produced. Organ 52 closed its last named piece: trace context now propagates across the Docker executor boundary (StructuredExecutorClient -> executor_service.py -> DockerRunner's spawned container, via new TraceContext.as_env()), proven at each hop via injected fakes since this sandbox has no live Docker daemon. Organs 30 and 46 were re-examined and confirmed unchanged: both remaining gaps (real production traffic to measure the human-state classifier against; the 9 named adversarial simulations actually running against a live system) are genuinely not fabricatable in an autonomous session, not wiring gaps. Organ 32 benefits incidentally from organ 31's new store (both its entrances now durably record evidence) but its own named gap -- wiring chat/the forge through the gateway, and reconciling the two other competing gateway implementations -- was deliberately not attempted, matching every prior pass's own risk assessment of the single most heavily used endpoint in the system without a live browser session to verify no regression. Counts unchanged: 40/14 (no organ's status field flipped in this pass -- every change narrowed a real blocker with new evidence rather than claiming a bar this pass could not fully verify, e.g. CI has not yet run on this exact commit).
